feedback:
## ATX Review — Issue #615 GUI Role Selector (Iteration 2)

**Aggregate rating: 2/5 — blocking issues remain**

---

### Blocking Issues

| # | Specialist | File | Issue |
|---|-----------|------|-------|
| B1 | test-coverage | `tests/cli/clawctl/provider/test_agent_attach_hermes_multi.py:207-236` | CLI idempotent re-attach test only checks `exit_code == 0` and `'already attached' in output`. No artifact read-back (`agent provider get -o json`) to confirm the stored provider list is still length 1. A silent duplicate-write bug would pass this test. GUI route counterpart correctly asserts state; CLI fix is incomplete by the same standard. **Fix**: after the second attach, invoke `agent provider get --agent sage-hermes -o json` and assert `len(json.loads(listed.output)) == 1`. |
| B2 | test-coverage | `tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:306-307` | 409 rebind test asserts `status_code == 409` and `'detach' in detail.lower()` but does NOT pin the conflicting slot name. A refactor that strips the slot name from the message still passes. **Fix**: add `assert 'primary' in exc.value.detail`. |
| B3 | lifecycle/backend | `src/clawrium/gui/routes/providers.py:572-577` | B2 backend fix is incomplete. Same-provider rebind → 409 ✅. But different-provider-same-slot (e.g. `provider-B` attaches to `vision` already held by `provider-A`) falls through to `_persist_attachments` → `validate()` → `AttachmentError` → HTTP **400**. The `_persist_attachments` docstring explicitly cements 400 for this path. **Fix**: add an explicit pre-`_persist_attachments` slot-conflict check: `occupied = next((e for e in current if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get('role') == body.role), None)` — if `occupied and _attachment_name(occupied) != name`, raise `HTTPException(409, detail=f'{body.role!r} is already bound to ...; detach first')`. Do NOT route through `AttachmentError`. |

---

### Warnings

| # | Specialist | File | Issue |
|---|-----------|------|-------|
| W1 | gui-frontend | `agent-attachments-card.tsx:178` | Non-in-flight detach rows stay visually enabled while another row is detaching; clicks hit the `if (detaching) return` guard silently. Fix: `disabled={detachDisabled \|\| !!detaching}`. |
| W2 | gui-frontend | `agent-attachments-card.tsx:174` | `<span title>` on a non-interactive wrapper is not announced by AT (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver). Fix: `aria-describedby` + `sr-only` span OR `aria-disabled="true"` with reason in `aria-label`. |
| W3 | lifecycle/backend | `providers.py:524-526` | TOCTOU: idempotent check and slot-conflict detection run against a stale `current` snapshot outside `update_host`'s lock. Concurrent attaches to different slots can silently lose one write. Same pattern in CLI `provider.py:97,242`. Move the read-check-write inside the lock. |
| W4 | lifecycle/backend | `providers.py:538-542` | 409 body is free-form prose, not structured JSON. Frontend must regex-parse to extract conflicting role/provider. Fix: return `{'message': '...', 'conflicting_role': ..., 'conflicting_provider': ...}`. |
| W5 | test-coverage | `tests/cli/clawctl/provider/test_agent_attach_hermes_multi.py:357-373` | The added "non-hermes detach" test actually covers non-hermes **attach** (with `--role` flag) — different operation. The non-hermes **detach** code path on the CLI still has no test. |
| W6 | test-coverage | `tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:310-319` | All-aux-filled is covered only at the `_available_roles()` unit level. No HTTP round-trip POST with all 9 slots pre-filled to exercise `validate()` → `AttachmentError` → HTTP 400 with slot name in body. |
| W7 | test-coverage | `tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:151-155` | W5 partial: `test_attachments_unknown_agent_404` still has no body assertion (only `status_code == 404`). |
| W8 | cli-typer | `src/clawrium/cli/clawctl/agent/_shared.py:102` | Pre-existing: `clawctl agent get` / `agent describe` shows only first provider for hermes. Not a regression; `agent provider get --agent <n>` is the canonical surface. Document in PR body. |

---

### Suggestions

| # | Specialist | File | Suggestion |
|---|-----------|------|------------|
| S1 | gui-frontend | `gui/src/lib/types.ts:237` | Add `ProviderRole` literal union type and annotate `ProviderAttachment.role` and `available_roles: ProviderRole[]`. Document lockstep with `AUXILIARY_SLOTS` in a comment. |
| S2 | lifecycle/backend | `provider_attachments.py:162-166` | `validate()` error names the slot but not the blocking provider. Thread the occupying provider name into the message for self-contained diagnostics. |
| S3 | lifecycle/backend | `providers.py:429-451` | Factor out `_find_slot_occupant(attachments, role) -> str | None` from `_available_roles` and reuse in the POST attach path to keep the conflict-detection logic DRY. |
| S4 | test-coverage | `tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:87-89` | Add a comment to `test_available_roles_empty_hermes_returns_primary_only` noting the W1 deferral so the "wrong" behavior isn't mistaken for the intended contract. |
| S5 | test-coverage | (missing) | Add `test_hermes_aux_slot_full_lifecycle`: attach primary → attach vision aux → list (assert aux_count=1, vision not in available_roles) → detach aux → list (assert aux_count=0, vision in available_roles). |

---

### Contract Parity Status

**Confirmed** by gui-frontend reviewer: `available_roles` is server-driven from `AUXILIARY_SLOTS`; no frontend hardcoding of the role list. The TS side accepts whatever the server returns. The role *type* is still untyped `string` (S1), but there is no hardcoded divergence risk at runtime.

The all-nine auxiliary slots (`vision`, `web_extract`, `compression`, `session_search`, `skills_hub`, `approval`, `mcp`, `title_generation`, `curator`) flow correctly server → client.

---

### Deferred Items — Status Confirmed

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| Backend W1 (first-attach-must-be-primary) | Deferred — issue body requires it; test `test_available_roles_empty_hermes_returns_primary_only` pins current behavior as passing (add deferral comment per S4) |
| Backend W2 (400 vs 422) | Deferred — no HTTP contract in issue body |
| Backend W4 (sync_required) | Deferred — out of scope; CLI doesn't surface it |

---

### Iteration 2 Summary

Three blockers must be resolved before merge: CLI idempotent test needs artifact read-back (B1), 409 rebind test must pin the slot name (B2), and the backend 409 for different-provider-same-slot is still returning 400 (B3). The contract parity goal is met at the data layer but the 409 contract is incomplete on the backend side. Recommend a targeted iteration 3 addressing B1–B3 and at minimum W5–W7 (test gaps).

<output>
{
  "rating": 2,
  "summary": "Two blockers remain: the CLI-level B1 idempotent-attach fix is incomplete (status-code only, no artifact read-back), and the B2 rebind-409 test does not pin the conflicting slot name in the response body. Three warnings cover a W3 mislabelling (attach-rejection test shipped instead of detach test), a missing HTTP-level all-aux-filled 409 round-trip, and a surviving agent-not-found 404 with no body assertion.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "blocking",
      "location": "tests/cli/clawctl/provider/test_agent_attach_hermes_multi.py:207-236",
      "issue": "B1 (CLI) — `test_hermes_attach_idempotent_same_role` asserts `exit_code == 0` and `'already attached' in result.output` only. It never calls `agent provider get --agent sage-hermes -o json` to verify the stored provider list is still length 1. A silent duplicate-write bug in `_set_attachments` (e.g. the early-return path returning before emitting the idempotent message) would pass this test. The GUI route counterpart at test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:268 is correct — it checks `resp['already_attached'] is True` AND `state['host']['agents']['sage']['providers'] == [...]`. The CLI fix is incomplete by the same standard.",
      "suggestion": "After the second `runner.invoke` attach, invoke `runner.invoke(app, ['agent', 'provider', 'get', '--agent', 'sage-hermes', '-o', 'json'])` and assert `len(json.loads(listed.output)) == 1` to confirm no duplicate was written."
    },
    {
      "severity": "blocking",
      "location": "tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:306-307",
      "issue": "B2 — `test_attach_hermes_rebind_without_detach_rejected` asserts `exc.value.status_code == 409` and `'detach' in exc.value.detail.lower()`. The production code at providers.py:537-543 includes `existing_role` in the detail: `f\"provider 'anth' already attached to agent 'sage' with role 'primary'; detach first to rebind\"`. The test does NOT assert that `'primary'` (the conflicting slot name) appears in the detail. A refactor that drops the slot name from the message still passes the test. The review question explicitly asked whether the 409 body pins the conflicting aux slot name — it does not.",
      "suggestion": "Add `assert 'primary' in exc.value.detail` (the existing_role at the time of conflict) to pin the slot name in the 409 body."
    },
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "tests/cli/clawctl/provider/test_agent_attach_hermes_multi.py:357-373",
      "issue": "W3 mislabelled — the stated fix is 'non-hermes detach test added (detaching from an agent type that doesn't support multi-provider must fail cleanly)'. What was actually added is `test_non_hermes_rejects_role_flag` which tests ATTACH with a `--role` flag on an openclaw agent — a different operation entirely. Iter-1 W3 was specifically about the non-hermes DETACH path exercising `normalize() → list[str]` on the delete surface. The GUI route has `test_detach_non_hermes_singleton_succeeds` (line 458) covering that path at the route level, but the CLI has no non-hermes detach test. The providers field is absent on `wise-hypatia` in conftest, so the case 'agent type has no providers field at all' is also uncovered at the CLI detach surface.",
      "suggestion": "Add a CLI test that calls `agent provider detach <name> --agent wise-hypatia` after a prior attach and verifies the provider is removed. Also cover the 'provider not attached' error case to exercise the emit_error path on a non-hermes detach."
    },
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:310-319",
      "issue": "W4 (HTTP level) — `test_available_roles_all_aux_filled_returns_empty` is a unit test on `_available_roles()` only. No POST to `attach_provider_to_agent` is attempted with all 9 aux slots pre-filled, so the path through `_persist_attachments` → `validate()` → `AttachmentError` → HTTP 400 is not exercised for the all-slots-full boundary. The nearest test (`test_attach_hermes_duplicate_aux_rejected` at line 234) fills only one slot. The HTTP error body for the full-fleet case is untested.",
      "suggestion": "Add a test that pre-fills all 9 AUXILIARY_SLOTS in the fixture and calls `attach_provider_to_agent` for a 10th aux provider; assert `status_code == 400` and that the slot name appears in `exc.value.detail`."
    },
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:151-155",
      "issue": "W5 partial fix — iter-1 W5 was 'provider-not-found 404 tests omit detail assertions'. The fix added `'ghost' in exc.value.detail` to `test_attach_unknown_provider_404` (line 256) and `test_detach_unknown_provider_404` (line 424). But `test_attachments_unknown_agent_404` at line 151 still asserts only `exc.value.status_code == 404` with no body assertion — the agent-not-found 404 path was missed.",
      "suggestion": "Add `assert 'missing' in exc.value.detail` (or the literal agent name) to `test_attachments_unknown_agent_404`."
    },
    {
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "location": "tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py (missing)",
      "issue": "No test exercises the full attach → `list_agent_attachments` → detach lifecycle for a single auxiliary slot with state assertions at each step. Individual operations are tested in isolation but sequential state integrity (attach writes role, list reflects it, detach removes it) has no single covering test.",
      "suggestion": "Add `test_hermes_aux_slot_full_lifecycle`: attach primary, attach vision aux, call `list_agent_attachments` and assert `aux_count == 1` and `available_roles` excludes vision, detach aux, call list again and assert `aux_count == 0` and vision reappears in `available_roles`."
    },
    {
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "location": "tests/test_gui_route_provider_attach.py:87-89",
      "issue": "Deferred W1 from iter-1 is baked in as passing test. `test_available_roles_empty_hermes_returns_primary_only` pins `_available_roles('hermes', []) == ['primary']`, which contradicts the spec documented in iter-1 W1 (available_roles must never include primary; primary availability is signalled by `primary_attached: false`). The deferred status is acceptable if intentional, but the test name 'returns_primary_only' presents the wrong behavior as the intended contract. At minimum annotate the test with a comment that this is a known spec deviation pending a follow-up.",
      "suggestion": "Add a comment: `# W1 deferred — spec says available_roles must not include 'primary'; frontend gates primary-attach on primary_attached: false. See iter-1 W1.`"
    }
  ],

    "Add a CLI non-hermes detach test (the true W3 fix) — the test added in iter-2 covers attach-with-role rejection, not the detach code path.",
    "Add an HTTP-level all-aux-filled 409 round-trip test, not just a unit test on `_available_roles`.",

  ],

    "files_reviewed": 6,

<output>
{
  "rating": 4,
  "summary": "Iteration 2 successfully addressed ATX iter-1 blockers (B1, B2) and most warnings. The GUI provider attachment surface is now functionally complete with role-selector parity. The CLI surface itself is clean—no regressions introduced. However, there's one CLI UX gap predating this iteration: the fleet/detail commands show only the first provider for multi-provider agents, requiring operators to use the dedicated provider get command to see all attachments. This is a pre-existing design choice, not a defect introduced here, but worth documenting as it affects CLI/GUI parity perception.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "src/clawrium/cli/clawctl/agent/_shared.py:102",
      "issue": "W1: `clawctl agent get` and `clawctl agent describe <name>` show only the first attached provider name for multi-provider (hermes) agents. Both commands call `agent_to_row()` which uses `_first_provider()` (line 111), returning only `claw_record['providers'][0]`. For hermes agents with primary + auxiliary providers, the PROVIDER column/field shows only the primary name; roles and auxiliary attachments are invisible. Operators must run the separate `clawctl agent provider get --agent <name>` command (provider.py:317-372) to see the full attachment list with roles. This creates a CLI UX inconsistency: the GUI agent detail overview tab (via AgentAttachmentsCard) shows all providers, but the CLI fleet/detail views do not.",
      "suggestion": "Document this as a known CLI limitation in the PR body or issue comments. The dedicated `clawctl agent provider get --agent <name>` command is the canonical surface for full provider detail; the fleet/describe views are intentionally compact. If full parity is desired, consider adding a --wide flag to `clawctl agent describe` that renders a multi-line Providers section (analogous to Skills/Channels), or add a hint in the Provider field when len(providers) > 1 (e.g., 'anth (+2 more)')."
    },
    {
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "location": "AGENTS.md / issue #615 acceptance criteria",
      "issue": "S1: The issue acceptance criteria don't explicitly call out which CLI commands should reflect multi-provider state. The CLI review focus in the prompt assumes `clawctl agent get` would show all attachments, but the actual CLI contract (from subtask 1, #612) only mandated `clawctl agent provider attach/detach/get --agent` parity. The get/describe commands' behavior isn't specified. This ambiguity could lead to future confusion about what 'CLI parity' means.",
      "suggestion": "When documenting multi-provider features in future issues, explicitly enumerate which CLI commands surface the attachment list: fleet view (`agent get`), detail view (`agent describe`), and provider-specific view (`agent provider get --agent`). Clarify which views are compact (first-only) vs. exhaustive."
    }
  ],
  "recommendations": [
    "✅ CLI output for configure/sync: The `gateway_token_rotated` event handler (sync.py:338-372) is untouched by GUI changes. Yellow notice rendering for zeroclaw bearer rotation remains intact.",
    "⚠️ CLI agent get/describe: Shows only first provider for hermes. This is a pre-existing CLI design choice (compact display), not a regression. The dedicated `clawctl agent provider get --agent <name>` command shows the full list with roles. Document this split in the PR body so operators understand the CLI surface.",
    "✅ Error surfacing: CLI and GUI operate independently on the same data layer (hosts.json). CLI uses `emit_error()` with exit code 1; GUI uses HTTPException. Error messages are structurally consistent (e.g., primary-detach 409 hints are similar). No raw JSON dumps reach CLI users.",
    "✅ Contract completeness (deferred items): W2 (400 vs 422) and W4 (sync_required) are correctly deferred for CLI scope. The CLI doesn't consume the GUI API; these are GUI-only concerns. The deferred W1-be (_available_roles logic) doesn't affect the CLI—validated by inspection of provider.py.",
    "✅ No B-level issues: No blocking defects in the CLI surface introduced or left unaddressed by this iteration. The GUI routes correctly mirror CLI semantics for attach/detach/role validation."
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "files_reviewed": 9,
    "domain": "cli-typer / GUI-CLI contract parity (hermes multi-provider #615)"
  }
}
</output>

<output>
{
  "rating": 3,
  "summary": "Iter-1 blockers are all correctly resolved. The span-wrapper tooltip fix eliminates nested interactive elements and the in-flight detach lock properly clears in both success and error paths via `finally`. Two warnings remain: other-row detach buttons stay visually enabled during an in-flight operation but silently no-op on click, and the span `title` tooltip is inaccessible to screen readers and keyboard-only users. The S1 attachment-array union fix is correct; the role field itself is still untyped. No new blocking issues found.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "gui/src/components/providers/agent-attachments-card.tsx:178",
      "issue": "Silent click-noop on non-in-flight rows during detach. `disabled={detachDisabled || detaching === row.name}` only disables the row currently being detached. While row A is detaching, rows B and C render as enabled buttons; clicking them hits the `if (detaching) return` guard at line 90 with zero feedback to the user. From the operator's perspective, the button appears active but does nothing.",
      "suggestion": "Change the condition to `disabled={detachDisabled || !!detaching}`. This disables every Detach button while any one is in-flight, matching the visual contract. The per-row `detaching === row.name` guard for the 'Detaching…' label can stay as-is."
    },
    {
      "severity": "warning",
      "location": "gui/src/components/providers/agent-attachments-card.tsx:174",
      "issue": "Span `title` tooltip is invisible to AT. `<span title={detachTitle}>` wrapping a disabled button fixes the Firefox/Safari hover-tooltip regression (the W1 fix intent), but `title` on a non-interactive `<span>` is not announced by NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver. A keyboard-only user who tabs to the disabled 'Detach' button hears 'Detach, dimmed, button' with no reason why it is disabled.",
      "suggestion": "Add `aria-describedby` on the `<Button>` pointing to a `<span id={...} className='sr-only'>` containing the reason text, OR — since the button is disabled — render it as `aria-disabled='true'` (not the HTML `disabled` attribute) with the reason in `aria-label` so it remains focusable and announced. Either approach lets AT users hear 'Detach auxiliary attachments first' without re-introducing interactive nesting."
    },
    {
